benefits: Maintains high promotion standard — only exceptional performers promoted
risks: Zero promotions creates talent stagnation risk for high performers
Ana Silva and John Smith (Ready for Promotion) may seek external advancement
Paula Johnson Medium Risk retention concern unaddressed by lack of promotion path
Carlos Santos despite Excellent performance and High Engagement receives no recognition

benefits: Recognizes 4 out of 5 employees — broad talent acknowledgment
Directly addresses retention risk for Paula Johnson
Accelerates career paths for John Smith and Ana Silva (Ready for Promotion)
Motivates Carlos Santos despite not yet meeting certifications threshold under conservative
risks: 80% promotion rate may dilute promotion prestige
Annual cost increase of 44,640 currency units
John Smith promotion while Burnout Risk is active may compound overload perception
Promoting 4 simultaneously may create compression issues with existing senior staff

final_recommendation.rationale: Neither scenario is optimal as-is. Conservative produces zero promotions — untenable for talent retention given two Ready-for-Promotion employees and one Medium Risk. Pure Aggressive promotes 80% which dilutes value. Recommended approach: promote E001 Ana Silva and E004 John Smith (both Ready for Promotion, strongest justification) immediately. Defer E002 Carlos Santos until certification milestone is reached (actionable within 90 days). Address E005 Paula Johnson through career development plan rather than promotion — her retention risk is path-related, not performance-related.
immediate_promotions: E004 John Smith (after burnout risk resolution)
alternative_retention_action: E005 Paula Johnson (career development plan, not promotion at this stage)
